response.justification: Candidate has strong geospatial domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work at Sociomantic, but the core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is a major gap. C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2007-2008, nearly two decades ago, and there is no Rust experience listed. Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which does not map cleanly to the routing-tiling pipeline stack.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS experience building map data platforms
Low-latency, high-throughput service experience (millions of req/s at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development
concerns: No modern C++ experience; C/C++ work is ~18 years old and entry-level
No Rust experience listed anywhere in the resume
Primary recent stack (TypeScript/Node/DLang) does not align with the role's systems-language focus
